creator: bsdcode@disroot.org
text:
Created attachment 248553
patch

I realized the music playback doesn't work with the OpenAL backend in version 0.9.2, at least in Baldur's Gate. The music plays at the titelscreen (and only before going "in-game") but nowhere else. Other sounds work. The SDL2_mixer backend doesn't have this problem.

It seems to be fixed already upstream, because I build a recent git checkout and the music plays just fine with OpenAL. Until upstream releases 0.9.3 it would be good to provide the option to use the SDL2_mixer backend for 0.9.2 users, although the SDL2_mixer backend has its own set of problems: it is a little bit unstable (I experienced crashes) and uses a fixed 2K buffer which introduces big audio latency. At least users have the option to pick their poison now.

I used the opportunity and extended my previous patch:

- use GCC (https://github.com/gemrb/gemrb/issues/1786)
- add optional OpenGL and SDL2_mixer support
- add disabled pixelscaling to screensize option
- change GemRB.cfg into a proper conffile
  (remove from pkg-plist and annotate GemRB.cfg.sample with @sample)
- make PNG, OGG, TTF and OpenAL support optional
- use compiler:c++11-lang
- remove gnome and localbase uses
- remove DOC_DIR and MAN_DIR from CMAKE_ARGS
- set CMake options explicitly
- use CMake options helpers for boolean options
- organize options into AUDIO, RESOURCE and VIDEO groups

Regarding the removal of USES=gnome localbase: I removed them because it builds without errors or warnings without specifying them. If there's another reason why they should be added back then let me know.

Regarding the removal of DOC_DIR and MAN_DIR: they have the desired value by default, therefore I removed them. If there's a reason why they should still be specified then let me know.
